title: Show-CronAiAthonTool ForeColor null Color error on prereq and bug count labels
description: Show-CronAiAthonTool.ps1 lines 766 and 976 assign .ForeColor using inline if-expressions: '$lblPreReqSummary.ForeColor = if ($result.allPassed) { $accGreen } else { $accRed }' and '$lblBugCount.ForeColor = if (@($bugs).Count -eq 0) { $accGreen } else { $accOrange }'. If $result is null (prereq check failed to return), $result.allPassed throws under StrictMode, and the if-expression returns $null which cannot be assigned to System.Drawing.Color. Same for $bugs: if the scan threw, $bugs may be null and even with @() wrapping the .Count is safe, but the real risk is StrictMode bleed from ShowDialog (P024) making the color variables inaccessible.
function_name: Show-CronAiAthonTool (prereq check handler, bug scan handler)
resolution: Lines 766 and 976 refactored to pre-evaluate if-expression into $preFc/$bugFc with null guard before ForeColor assignment.
detection_method: User-reported 'Cannot convert null to type System.Drawing.Color' error in Cron-Ai-Athon Tool context. Grep scan confirmed inline if-expression assignments at lines 766 and 976.
root_cause: Inline if-expression used to assign .ForeColor value-type property. If the condition throws, the expression returns $null which .NET cannot cast to System.Drawing.Color.
